fact: May 5 is exactly 125 days into the year - that's like counting to 125 on your fingers and toes more than 12 times!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is typically prime time for baby animals to be born because the weather is getting warmer and there's more food around.
Cherry blossom petals are often falling like pink snow in many parts of Asia around this time, creating magical carpets on the ground.
The sun usually rises around 6 AM and sets around 7:30 PM in most of North America, giving you almost 13.5 hours of daylight to play outside!
People born on May 5 are Taurus bulls - they're supposedly stubborn but also really good at finishing things they start.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, leaves are typically turning orange and red because it's actually autumn there!
Dandelions are often exploding into those fluffy white puffballs you can blow on to make wishes - and they're spreading their seeds everywhere.
May 5 falls almost exactly halfway between the spring equinox and summer solstice in the north - it's like the perfect middle of spring!
Migrating butterflies like monarchs are typically flying north around this time, sometimes traveling over 100 miles in a single day.
In many temperate places, this is when morning dew starts getting really heavy because warm days meet cool nights - everything looks like it's covered in tiny diamonds.
The air temperature can swing wildly on May 5 - it might be 45°F in the morning and 75°F by afternoon in temperate zones.
